map.condition: Adding two N-bit numbers, ripple carry
MUX implementing a Boolean function of n variables
Decoder implementing any m-output combinational function

formulas.name: Half Adder
formulas.latex: S = A \oplus B, \quad C_{out} = A \cdot B
formulas.plain: S = A XOR B, C_out = A AND B
formulas.whenToUse: Adding two single bits with no carry-in

terms.means: Sum bit
Carry out
Input bits

formulas.trap: Half adder cannot accept a carry-in; use full adder for cascading stages

formulas.name: Full Adder
formulas.latex: S = A \oplus B \oplus C_{in}, \quad C_{out} = A \cdot B + C_{in}(A \oplus B)
formulas.plain: S = A XOR B XOR Cin, C_out = AB + Cin(A XOR B)
formulas.whenToUse: Adding two bits plus a carry-in; the building block of ripple and lookahead adders

terms.means: Carry into the current bit position
Propagate term P
Generate term G

formulas.trap: 2's complement addition with N bits still needs N full adders — the sign bit is treated like any other bit

formulas.name: Ripple Carry Adder Delay
formulas.latex: t_{ripple} = N \cdot t_{FA}
formulas.plain: t_ripple = N * t_FA
formulas.whenToUse: Estimating worst-case delay of an N-bit ripple carry adder

terms.means: Number of bit positions
Delay of one full adder stage

formulas.trap: Ripple carry delay grows linearly with N; do not confuse with O(log N) of CLA

formulas.name: Generate and Propagate Terms
formulas.latex: G_i = A_i \cdot B_i, \quad P_i = A_i \oplus B_i
formulas.plain: G_i = A_i AND B_i, P_i = A_i XOR B_i
formulas.whenToUse: Defining the fundamental CLA building blocks for each bit position i

terms.means: Carry generate at bit i (carry produced regardless of carry-in)
Carry propagate at bit i (passes carry-in to carry-out)

formulas.trap: Some texts define P_i = A_i OR B_i (used in group generate/propagate); the XOR form is used for sum computation

formulas.name: CLA Carry Expressions
formulas.latex: C_{i+1} = G_i + P_i \cdot C_i
formulas.plain: C_{i+1} = G_i + P_i * C_i
formulas.whenToUse: Expanding carry equations in a carry lookahead adder (unfold to eliminate ripple)

terms.means: Carry into bit position i
Generate and propagate at bit i

formulas.trap: Unfolding to C3 requires substituting C2, C1, C0 recursively; forgetting one level is a common error

formulas.name: CLA Adder Delay
formulas.latex: t_{CLA}(N) = O(\log N)
formulas.plain: t_CLA(N) = O(log N)
formulas.whenToUse: Comparing CLA speed against ripple carry; multi-level CLA (e.g. 74181 + 74182) achieves this

terms.means: Total number of bits

formulas.trap: A single 4-bit CLA chip is O(1) delay; the log factor appears only in multi-level (hierarchical) CLA for large N

formulas.name: Group Generate and Propagate (3-bit example)
formulas.whenToUse: Computing the carry-out of a group of bits in a hierarchical CLA (used in 74182-style circuits)

terms.means: Group generate for bits 2 down to 0
Group propagate for bits 2 down to 0

formulas.trap: The group generate expression depends on the order of expansion; always expand from the MSB inward

formulas.name: Array Multiplier Structure
formulas.latex: \text{Partial products: } p_{ij} = a_i \cdot b_j, \quad 0 \le i,j \le n-1
formulas.plain: Partial products: p_ij = a_i AND b_j, for 0 <= i,j <= n-1
formulas.whenToUse: Understanding how an n×n array multiplier is constructed from AND gates and adder rows

terms.means: Individual bits of the two n-bit operands
Bit of the partial product matrix

formulas.trap: The partial product matrix has n rows each of n bits; the array has n-1 adder rows (not n)

formulas.name: Array Multiplier Gate Delay
formulas.latex: t_{array}(n) = \Theta(n)
formulas.plain: t_array(n) = Theta(n)
formulas.whenToUse: Answering GATE questions about the delay complexity of an n×n array multiplier

terms.means: Bit-width of each operand

formulas.trap: The delay is Theta(n), NOT Theta(n^2). The area (number of gates) is Theta(n^2); do not confuse delay with area

formulas.name: N-to-1 MUX Output Expression
formulas.latex: Y = \sum_{i=0}^{N-1} I_i \cdot m_i, \quad N = 2^n
formulas.plain: Y = sum over i of (I_i AND m_i), where N = 2^n and m_i is the minterm of select lines
formulas.whenToUse: Deriving the Boolean output of a MUX circuit from its select lines and data inputs

terms.means: Data input at position i
Minterm of the n select-line variables corresponding to index i
Number of select lines

formulas.trap: Select line order (MSB vs LSB) determines which data input is selected; reversing the order flips outputs

formulas.name: MUX as Universal Combinational Element
formulas.latex: 2^{n-1}\text{-to-}1 \text{ MUX can implement any } n\text{-variable Boolean function}
formulas.plain: A 2^(n-1)-to-1 MUX can implement any n-variable Boolean function (one variable used to drive data inputs)
formulas.whenToUse: Choosing MUX size when implementing an arbitrary Boolean function

terms.means: Number of Boolean input variables
Number of data inputs required (one variable drives data lines)

formulas.trap: A 2^n-to-1 MUX (connecting variables only to select lines, data lines tied to 0/1) also works but is unnecessarily large

formulas.name: 4-to-1 MUX Equation
formulas.whenToUse: Evaluating the output of a 4-to-1 MUX given select and data inputs

terms.means: Select lines (S1 = MSB)
Data inputs

formulas.trap: Always verify which select bit is MSB; many GATE diagrams label S0 as MSB, causing an off-by-one in the output

formulas.name: n-to-2^n Decoder
formulas.latex: n \text{ inputs} \Rightarrow 2^n \text{ outputs, output } O_i = m_i \text{ (minterm } i\text{)}
formulas.plain: n inputs => 2^n outputs; output O_i is the minterm m_i of the n input variables
formulas.whenToUse: Implementing any combinational function using a decoder and OR gates

terms.means: Minterm i of the n input variables
Output line i (asserted when inputs equal binary i)

formulas.trap: Decoders generate ALL minterms; to implement a function OR together the minterms in its SOP canonical form

formulas.name: Implementing m Functions with Decoder
formulas.latex: f_j(x_1,\ldots,x_n) = \sum_{\text{minterms of } f_j} O_i \quad (\text{one OR gate per output})
formulas.plain: f_j = OR of selected O_i outputs from the decoder (one OR gate per output function f_j)
formulas.whenToUse: Designing a multiple-output combinational block using one decoder

terms.means: One of the m output functions

formulas.trap: Need NAND decoder? Use NAND gates instead of OR — each output is NOR/NAND form; verify active-high vs active-low

formulas.name: ROM Size for a Combinational Function
formulas.latex: \text{ROM size} = 2^n \times m \text{ bits}
formulas.plain: ROM size = 2^n x m bits (n = address/input bits, m = data/output bits)
formulas.whenToUse: Sizing a ROM to implement a combinational function with n inputs and m outputs

terms.means: Number of input (address) lines
Number of output (data) lines
Number of address locations (one per minterm)

formulas.trap: ROM implements the complete truth table; do not try to minimize — all 2^n rows must be stored

formulas.name: ROM Internal Structure
formulas.latex: \text{ROM} = n\text{-to-}2^n \text{ decoder (fixed AND plane)} + m\text{-bit OR plane (programmable)}
formulas.plain: ROM = fixed n-to-2^n decoder (AND plane) + programmable OR plane with m outputs
formulas.whenToUse: Explaining how a ROM realises any sum-of-minterms expression

terms.means: Decoder that generates all 2^n minterms
Programmable connections selecting which minterms contribute to each output

formulas.trap: In a PLA the AND plane is also programmable; in a ROM only the OR plane is programmable

formulas.name: Static Hazard Definition
formulas.latex: \text{Static-1 hazard: output should stay 1 but momentarily goes 0 during input transition}
formulas.plain: Static-1 hazard: output should stay 1 but glitches to 0 during a single-variable input change
formulas.whenToUse: Identifying glitches in a combinational circuit caused by unequal gate delays

terms.means: Output is 1 in both states but dips to 0 transiently
Output is 0 in both states but rises to 1 transiently

formulas.trap: Hazards occur during transitions between adjacent minterms (one variable changes); multi-variable transitions cause dynamic hazards

formulas.name: Static Hazard Elimination
formulas.latex: \text{Add a redundant consensus term covering the transition: } f = AB + A'C + BC
formulas.plain: Add redundant prime implicant BC that bridges the gap between the two original terms AB and A'C (consensus theorem)
formulas.whenToUse: Eliminating a static-1 hazard in a SOP expression by adding a redundant cover

terms.means: Consensus/redundant term that overlaps the two adjacent prime implicants

formulas.trap: Minimising with K-map alone may remove the bridging term, reintroducing the hazard; explicitly keep the redundant term

formulas.name: NAND-NAND = SOP
formulas.latex: \overline{\overline{AB} \cdot \overline{CD}} = AB + CD
formulas.plain: NAND(NAND(A,B), NAND(C,D)) = AB + CD  (two-level NAND equals SOP)
formulas.whenToUse: Converting a two-level NAND circuit to its equivalent Boolean SOP expression

terms.means: NAND of X and Y

formulas.trap: NOR-NOR implements POS, not SOP; confusing NAND-NAND with NOR-NOR is the classic GATE pitfall

formulas.name: NOR-NOR = POS
formulas.latex: \overline{\overline{A+B} + \overline{C+D}} = (A+B)(C+D)
formulas.plain: NOR(NOR(A,B), NOR(C,D)) = (A+B)(C+D)  (two-level NOR equals POS)
formulas.whenToUse: Converting a two-level NOR circuit to its equivalent Boolean POS expression

terms.means: NOR of X and Y

formulas.trap: A NOR-NOR structure is NOT equivalent to SOP; students often write sum form instead of product form

formulas.name: Open-Collector Wired-AND
formulas.latex: Z = \overline{f_1} \cdot \overline{f_2} \cdots \overline{f_k} = \overline{f_1 + f_2 + \cdots + f_k}
formulas.plain: Wired-AND of open-collector NAND outputs gives Z = NOT(f1 OR f2 OR ... OR fk)
formulas.whenToUse: Analysing circuits where NAND outputs with open collectors are tied together to a pull-up resistor

terms.means: Boolean function computed by the i-th NAND gate (before open-collector inversion)
Resulting wired output

formulas.trap: Totem-pole TTL outputs cannot be wired-AND; only open-collector or tri-state outputs allow bus sharing

formulas.name: Cross-Coupled NAND = SR Latch
formulas.latex: Q = \overline{S' + \overline{Q'}}, \quad \overline{Q} = \overline{R' + Q} \quad (\text{but in combinational analysis: if no memory, trace signal levels})
formulas.plain: Two cross-coupled NANDs form an SR latch; analyse by tracing stable signal values iteratively
formulas.whenToUse: Analysing NAND feedback circuits in GATE questions that ask for steady-state output given an input A

terms.means: Set and Reset active-low inputs (for NAND latch)

formulas.trap: A single cross-coupled NAND pair with one external input acts as an inverter with feedback — analyse stable state, not sequential clocking
